title: TTCL Semiotic Program — compiler L3 (SemioticDialect) input contract
description: A declarative TTCL program consumed by the @sovereign/compiler L3 SemioticDialect loader. Declares wheels, signs, and combinator ops; the compiler builds an SSA SignGraph, runs L2 passes (type/modality inference, constitution compliance, budgeted expansion), then the L1 Provenance pass (linear token threading, encodeSign/decodeSign lowering, KeyCap capability check) over the optional `provenance` section, and — on success — lowers to the @sovereign/ttcl runtime (L0) by instantiating Wheel/Sign objects. Validated at load time with ajv. The L2 combinator op set: compose (the TTCL JOIN), map / fold / choose (modality-preserving passthroughs in v1 — full branch-join semantics deferred). The L1 encoding ops (encodeSign / decodeSign) live in the optional `provenance.ops` array, NOT in `ops`. Sample programs: shared/fixtures/layer7/semiotic-program-{triadic,raw-technology}.json.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference (the schema's own $id). Permitted but not required.

wheels.description: Wheel declarations (Llull rotating wheels). The product of their sizes is checked against `budget` by the L2 budgeted-expansion pass via combineWheelsBudgeted.

signs.description: Sign declarations. Each is materialized via makeSign (the manifold is the sole source of truth for the peirce block). `mode` is the CoarseMode (ICON/INDEX/SYMBOL); `modality` is the modal-lattice position (PURE/ICON/INDEX/SYMBOL/HYBRID).

ops.description: Combinator ops (the L2 SignGraphDialect). Each op references prior SSA nodes by id. `compose` is the TTCL JOIN (output HYBRID); `map` is a unary carrier passthrough (structural identity at the IR level — the rewrite pass eliminates it); `fold`/`choose` are branching combinators whose inferred type is the lattice JOIN of all inputs (full branch-join semantics); `attachModality` overrides its single carrier's modality (requires `modality` + exactly one input — enforced by the `if`/`then` below).

inputs.description: Referenced SSA node ids (signs or prior ops).

modality.description: attachModality: the target modality to attach to the carrier (required for attachModality, ignored otherwise).

threshold.description: Optional constitution pass threshold override; defaults to CONSTITUTION_PASS_THRESHOLD from the canonical numerics.

constitution.description: Graph-level constitution declaration (the prose's L3 `defineConstitution` op, modeled as a graph field).

budget.description: Wheel state-space budget cap. The L2 budgeted-expansion pass throws WheelBudgetExceededError when ∏ wheel sizes > budget.

output.description: Terminal SSA node id (the program's output). Triadic closure is enforced on this node's inferred domains.

provenance.description: Optional L1 Provenance section (the Linear-types fundamental). Declares single-use Trithemius key capabilities (KeyCaps), linear provenance tokens (emitToken / mergeProvenance), and encoding ops (encodeSign / decodeSign) that lower to the runtime cipher. The L2 `ops` enum is unchanged — encodeSign/decodeSign are NOT accepted in `ops` (they live here). The compiler's L1 pass enforces linear token threading (one terminal token = the provenance root), a SYMBOL-modality check on encodeSign, and the KeyCap capability check (each key consumed exactly once). When absent, L1 is a no-op and the program lowers as before.

keyCaps.description: Trithemius key capability declarations. Each is single-use (consumed exactly once by one encodeSign/decodeSign op). `wheel` references a declared wheel; `alphabetSize` is the cipher alphabet (use 256 for byte-range signs).

tokens.description: Linear provenance tokens. `emitToken` anchors a token at an index; `mergeProvenance` joins input tokens into one merged lineage. Each token is consumed at most once (by a mergeProvenance input); exactly one token is the unconsumed terminal (the provenance root). Double-consumed or ambiguous-root tokens are L1 compile errors.

inputs.description: mergeProvenance referenced token ids (required for mergeProvenance).

ops.description: Encoding lowering ops. `encodeSign` reads a SYMBOL-modality sign + consumes a KeyCap, producing an opaque EncToken (the Trithemius ciphertext). `decodeSign` reads an EncToken + consumes a KeyCap, producing a recovered Sign. `wheel` is accepted for signature fidelity to encodeSign(s,w,k); the cipher key state lives in the KeyCap's wheel. These are the L1 dialect ops — distinct from the L2 combinator `ops` array.

sign.description: encodeSign: the sign-producing node id to encode — a `sign` declaration or an `op` output (e.g. an `attachModality` that promotes a non-SYMBOL sign to SYMBOL). Must infer to SYMBOL-modality (enforced by the L1 pass).

token.description: decodeSign: the EncToken-producing op id to decode (an encodeSign op).
